sources.title: Cal. Code Regs. Tit. 14, § 7.50(b)(152) — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tahoe Lake and tributaries)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: Cal. Code Regs. Tit. 14, § 7.50(a)(1) — General Provisions (waters listed in subsection (b) closed except during the open season shown)
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to all fishing except during the open season as shown.

sources.quotedText: Truckee River from the confluence of Trout Creek downstream to the mouth of Prosser Creek.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap — Trout Creek (way 1001316492): a traced segment of the named creek in South Lake Tahoe, El Dorado Co. (waterway=stream); representative point ~38.93673, -119.99427 (superseded by GNIS mouth point, see gnis_arcgis_streams_layer source)
USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 236569, "Trout Creek", Stream, CA, El Dorado Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":236569,"gaz_name":"Trout Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado","objectid_1":1402149}. Corrects prior file coordinates (-119.99427, 38.93673), which were off by ~518 m from GNIS's recorded mouth point (-119.996584, 38.941024) near the confluence with Lake Tahoe (Truckee Marsh, South Lake Tahoe); the file's prior coordinate appears to have been derived from an OpenStreetMap representative point along the creek's course rather than pulled from GNIS (the creek's headwater point south of Freel Peak, also in GNIS's multipoint record, is at approximately -119.913526, 38.833523).

groups.humanSummary: Trout Creek is a south-shore Lake Tahoe tributary (El Dorado Co.): it originates south of Freel Peak in the Carson Range and flows north through South Lake Tahoe into Lake Tahoe (via the Truckee Marsh near the Upper Truckee River mouth). It is not listed under its own name — the only 'Trout Creek' string in §7.50 is a locational reference in the Truckee River entry §7.50(b)(154)(B), naming a different Trout Creek near the town of Truckee. As a Lake Tahoe tributary it is governed by the special Tahoe-tributary regulation §7.50(b)(152)(A): catch-and-release open season Sat. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 0 trout; closed the remainder of the year per §7.50(a)(1). There is no lake on Trout Creek's main stem, so 'upstream to the first lake' covers the whole creek.
groups.verbatimText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

parameters.note: Trout Creek is a tributary of Lake Tahoe: it originates south of Freel Peak in the Carson Range and flows north through South Lake Tahoe into the lake (through the Truckee Marsh at the south shore, near the Upper Truckee River mouth). It is not listed by its own name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b) — the sole 'Trout Creek' string in §7.50 is a geographic reference point in the Truckee River listing §7.50(b)(154)(B), a different Trout Creek near the town of Truckee. This South Lake Tahoe Trout Creek falls under the Lake Tahoe tributaries entry §7.50(b)(152)(A), which applies to 'Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake' — here the whole creek, since no lake lies on the main stem. Per §7.50(a)(1), waters listed in subsection (b) are closed to all fishing except during the shown open season — so the creek is closed Oct. 1 through the Friday preceding the Saturday before Memorial Day. Date_spec encodes 'Saturday preceding Memorial Day' as the last Monday of May (Memorial Day) minus 2 days, since the resolver has no anchor_holiday implementation.

regulations.humanSummary: Open catch-and-release only, Saturday before Memorial Day through Sept. 30; closed to all fishing the rest of the year (Lake Tahoe tributary special regulation).
regulations.verbatimText: Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks only during the open window; no bait.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Trout Creek is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.); the Lake Tahoe CA/NV license reciprocity applies to fishing in the lake itself, not to this tributary.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
